title: Frank Giacoia
content:
{{Top|rwp}}
[[File:Frank_giacoia.jpg|right|100px]]
'''Frank Giacoia''' ([[1920s|1925]] – [[1989]]) was a comic book artist who inked an issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series.

Giacoia was the high-school classmate of fellow comic artist [[Carmine Infantino]], and the two worked together occasionally through the years. His early work was in the 1940s for various companies including [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC Comics]]. During the 1960s he inked [[Wikipedia:Jack Kirby|Jack Kirby's]] pencils on the popular [[Wikipedia:Captain America|''Captain America'']] series for [[Marvel Comics]]. Through the 1970s and 80s he worked on many newspaper strips including [[Wikipedia:Flash Gordon|''Flash Gordon'']] and [[Wikipedia:The Amazing Spider-Man|''The Amazing Spider-Man'']].
